module_mood_contract: Boris-flat technical register — no dedicated module mood contract. Supplementary opt-in: senior engineer reads to satisfy mechanism curiosity while the room finishes the MCP install. Expected emotional state: 'I now have a precise vocabulary for what I've been doing.' No arc obligation; does not need to set up the next module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary names agent, harness, loop — vocabulary AE101 uses throughout. Does not surface Huryn's three blocks, Ronacher's patterns, or M3 skill authoring. Safe from cross-module spoil.

Body implicitly surfaces theme 6 (non-agentic-default: 'an LLM that runs in a loop, calling tools' — defining agenticness structurally) and theme 4 (self-aware: tool-injection prompt asks Claude to explain how it knows its own tools). Two themes touched without naming them.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversations or trainer cues.
Forcing functions in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three prompts via registry markers do the teaching work. Body paragraph is scaffolding, not the lesson. Maintainer block confirms: 'The three prompts demonstrate the loop on the student's live session; that's the teaching move. The paragraph is scaffolding for the prompts, not the lesson.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; no over-lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Not a lecture file. Supplementary prework read; lecture placement rules don't apply.
Not a lecture; supplementary opt-in. N/A.

Single phase scored 7/10. This is a supplementary with no dedicated mood contract and no arc obligation. Applying the mood-floor carve-out from check_strategy_tie_in.md rule 1 and check_pedagogy.md rule 27 canonical source (2026-05-03): the entire file is a TEACHING beat (three live-session prompts making the loop concrete), not a setup/launch beat. Score 7 is the facilitator-premium gap — the 'Feel free to dive deeper on any aspect.' callout is vague and trails rather than lands. Below the 8 floor but above 7; this is a facilitator-premium signature rather than a blocking REVISE. No beat scores below 7.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace the trailing '> Feel free to dive deeper on any aspect.' callout with a one-line forward-hook that names a concrete next move — e.g. a fourth optional prompt, or a pointer to a specific reference page — so the close hands off rather than drifts open.

rules_evaluated.evidence: The three prompts make the loop concrete on the student's live session. Persona cannot skip the teaching moment — the prompts ARE the teaching move and they are the entire body below the scaffolding paragraph. The mechanism is demystified by execution, not by reading.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors its module mood contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block states Boris-flat technical voice. Opening paragraph is mechanism description, not framing or motivation. Consistent with AE101 voice contract: 'Boris (Cherny): factual, direct, technically precise. The platform-truth voice.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — no pre-empting the next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File is supplementary opt-in with no arc position. Does not resolve any module's teaching moment — it is vocabulary scaffolding, and the vocabulary (harness, loop, tool injection) is used throughout but never 'revealed' as a module's big move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off appropriately.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No arc obligation for a supplementary. The '> Feel free to dive deeper on any aspect.' callout is weak as a closer (scored 7 mood) but does not break any module arc. Facilitator-premium gap only.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Sharpen the closer to a specific next move. Current trailing callout leaves the student without a destination.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Three prompts via registry markers. All three prompt bodies (ae101-agent-loop-ascii, ae101-agent-loop-tools-list, ae101-agent-loop-tool-injection) contain no placeholder brackets. Direct action instructions only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The body text reads: 'Three prompts to make the loop concrete in your current session.' followed immediately by the three {{prompt:...}} markers with NO individual action lead-in per prompt. Rule 2 requires a one-sentence lead-in before each prompt block — what each individual prompt does. The single collective lead-in covers all three but each marker individually lacks the required one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one action lead-in sentence above each of the three {{prompt:...}} markers. Example: 'Ask Claude to draw the agent loop as an ASCII diagram, naming each component.' / 'List every tool available in this session.' / 'Ask Claude to explain how it knows which tools it can call, and how MCP connectors extend that set.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All three prompts use {{prompt:<key>}} registry marker syntax. Registry files ae101-agent-loop-ascii.md, ae101-agent-loop-tools-list.md, ae101-agent-loop-tool-injection.md all exist at curriculum/prompts/.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 1 (7/10): '> Feel free to dive deeper on any aspect.' closes the file as a trail-off rather than a hand-off. Persona walks away satisfied but without a clear next move. The three prompts do their job; the closer doesn't earn the close.
